# Port of etcd-raft's replicate_pause.txt.
# Regression test for https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/pull/14633.
#
# When a leader's in-flight tracker to a follower fills up, the MsgApp stream
# to that follower must be paused (Progress.msgAppFlowPaused = true). Without
# this, the leader would unbounded-fanout MsgApp regardless of inflight cap.

# 3 nodes with very small inflight capacity (3) so we can saturate it quickly.
add-nodes id=1,2,3 voters=1,2,3 inflight=3
----
INFO 1 switched to configuration voters=(1 2 3)
INFO 1 became follower at term 0
INFO 2 switched to configuration voters=(1 2 3)
INFO 2 became follower at term 0
INFO 3 switched to configuration voters=(1 2 3)
INFO 3 became follower at term 0

campaign id=1
----
INFO 1 is starting a new election
INFO 1 became StateCandidate at term 1

stabilize
----
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateCandidate term=1 commit=0
> 2 receiving MsgRequestVote from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 3 receiving MsgRequestVote from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 1 receiving MsgRequestVoteResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgRequestVoteResponse from 3
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=0
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=0
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=1
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3

# Propose 3 entries. With inflight=3, the leader's MsgApp tracking to peers
# 2 and 3 should fill up after the broadcast.
propose id=1 data=p12
----
ok

propose id=1 data=p13
----
ok

propose id=1 data=p14
----
ok

# Push the leader's Ready out (one Ready that broadcasts the 3 new entries
# in a single MsgApp each). Do NOT stabilize — we want to inspect the
# leader's progress map before any follower responds.
process-ready id=1
----
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=1

# Expect followers 2 and 3 to be paused with inflight at the limit.
# Note: in this simulator MsgApp batches several entries into one inflight
# slot (see Progress.sentEntries which calls inflights.add once per send),
# so the inflight count is 1 — but Inflights does have a byte-size limit too.
# What's important is that the leader has recorded an inflight to each
# follower and won't issue another MsgApp until it's acked.
progress id=1
----
1: StateReplicate match=4 next=5
2: StateReplicate match=1 next=5 paused inflight=3[full]
3: StateReplicate match=1 next=5 paused inflight=3[full]

# After followers ack, the inflights are freed and progress returns to a
# normal StateReplicate with no pending in-flight.
stabilize
----
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=1
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3
> 1 handling Ready: state=StateLeader term=1 commit=4
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 2 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=4
> 3 receiving MsgAppend from 1
> 3 handling Ready: state=StateFollower term=1 commit=4
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 2
> 1 receiving MsgAppendResponse from 3

progress id=1
----
1: StateReplicate match=4 next=5
2: StateReplicate match=4 next=5
3: StateReplicate match=4 next=5
